Portfolio Project · May 2026

Healthcare Fraud in America:
FCA Settlement Analysis

A multi-phase forensic data investigation into Medicare and Medicaid fraud using public CMS datasets, HHS OIG records, and DOJ enforcement data

Author Dawn Krysa, PA-C, MSHIIM, CHDA
Tools Python · SQL · pandas · SQLite · Matplotlib
Data Sources CMS PDUF · HHS OIG LEIE · DOJ FCA · Medicaid T-MSIS

Phases Complete
Phase 1

Medicaid T-MSIS Anomaly Detection

Applied Python outlier detection using pandas and MinMaxScaler across Medicaid T-MSIS billing data. Flagged providers whose billing patterns were mathematically impossible given real-world time and staffing constraints — a hallmark of claims inflation fraud.

Key Finding: Quality One Care Home Health (Silver Spring, MD) billed for 7,201 LPN hours in a single 672-hour month. Greater New York Home Care LLC billed 548 hours of LPN care per patient in a 31-day period. Both flagged and submitted to OIG as formal referrals.
ProviderNPILocationAnomalyStatus
Quality One Care Home Health Inc.1528351285Silver Spring, MD7,201 billed hrs in 672-hr monthOIG Referred
Greater New York Home Care LLC1225205354Brooklyn, NY548 hrs LPN care/patient (31-day month)OIG Referred
Phase 2

Medicare Physician Utilization Analysis — Arizona Cluster

Queried CMS Medicare Provider Utilization data via SQLite in Python to identify NP billing outliers. Discovered a 7-provider cluster in Arizona billing amniotic allograft injections at $453,000–$957,000 per patient, versus a national NP average of approximately $3,500 per patient.

Figure 1: Arizona amniotic allograft cluster — 7 providers, Medicare paid vs. patients treated. Bubble size proportional to spend per patient. 71% defendant confirmation rate via Phase 3 DOJ cross-reference.
Key Finding: 5 of 7 flagged providers were subsequently charged in 2024–2025 DOJ enforcement actions. The remaining 2 providers (Elisabeth Balken NP, Arlene Bautista NP) were submitted as OIG referrals in May 2026.
Phase 3

DOJ FCA Cross-Reference & Enforcement Validation

Cross-referenced Phase 1 and Phase 2 flagged providers against DOJ False Claims Act settlement and indictment records. Validated that independently flagged outliers matched providers already under federal investigation or charged — confirming the analytical methodology.

Validation Rate: 71% — 5 of 7 independently flagged Arizona cluster providers appeared in subsequent DOJ enforcement actions without prior knowledge of the investigation. This confirms the outlier detection approach surfaces real fraud, not statistical noise.

Phase 4

OIG LEIE National Exclusion Analysis — The Mississippi Crisis

Analyzed the full HHS OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) — 83,001 current exclusion records — to produce a national patient safety ranking across all 50 states. Discovered a statistically extreme outlier in Mississippi patient abuse exclusions that was not previously documented in published research.

Figure 2: All 50 states ranked by patient abuse exclusions per 100,000 residents. Mississippi isolated at top in red.
Figure 3: Mississippi patient abuse breakdown by facility type. 83% of ICF/IID exclusions are for patient abuse — facilities serving nonverbal, cognitively disabled adults.
Figure 4: Top 15 states by patient abuse exclusions per capita. Mississippi at 26.59 — 2.6× the #2 state (Louisiana/Delaware tied at 10.20).
Mississippi Finding: 26.59 patient abuse exclusions per 100,000 residents — 9.58% of all national patient abuse exclusions while comprising only 0.87% of the US population. Abuse is concentrated in ICF/IID facilities serving nonverbal and cognitively disabled patients who cannot self-report. During the COVID enforcement gap (2021), exclusions nationally dropped 31% — meaning cases involving these vulnerable patients sat unprocessed.
Figure 5: LEIE exclusion type breakdown. 1128b4 permissive felony (any crime) is the dominant category at 39.9%. Nurses and aides account for 72.2% of all permissive felony exclusions.
Figure 6: Federal healthcare exclusion volume 2019–2024. COVID enforcement trough (2021) at lowest point in 15 years. 2024 spike driven by DOJ takedown operations.
Methodology

Tools, Data Sources & Approach

All analysis performed using publicly available federal datasets. No proprietary data, no HIPAA-covered information. Full code available in the repository.

PhaseData SourceToolMethod
1 — T-MSIS AnomalyMedicaid T-MSIS Analytic Files (CMS)Python / pandasMinMaxScaler outlier detection on billing volume vs. time constraints
2 — Medicare UtilizationCMS Medicare Provider Utilization & Payment DataSQLite / PythonSQL aggregation; $/patient vs. specialty benchmark flagging
3 — DOJ Cross-ReferenceDOJ FCA Settlement & Press Release DatabasePython / pandasNPI and provider name matching against enforcement records
4 — OIG LEIE AnalysisHHS OIG List of Excluded Individuals/EntitiesPython / pandasPer-capita rate calculation; facility type classification; state ranking
